issues.description: FR-3b default behavior ambiguity: The spec says sccache defaults apply when the '[cache]' section is 'absent entirely,' but does not specify behavior when '[cache]' is present with an empty or missing '[cache.env]' sub-table. Three plausible interpretations: (1) empty env = no cache env vars at all, (2) empty env = sccache defaults, (3) presence of '[cache]' without 'disabled = true' but without 'env' is a config error. This will produce different implementations depending on interpretation.
issues.suggestion: Add explicit language: 'If [cache] is present but [cache.env] is absent or empty, no cache env vars are injected (the sccache defaults only apply when the entire [cache] section is missing). To get sccache behavior, explicitly list the sccache env vars in [cache.env].' Or choose interpretation (2) if backward-compat matters more.

issues.description: FR-6b disk usage requires a running pod with /cache mounted, but no agent pods may be running when 'nemo cache show' or 'GET /dashboard/cache' is called. The spec says 'du -sh /cache/* from the latest agent pod (via existing pod-exec introspection, or just a one-shot kubectl exec on the most recent terminal pod)' but doesn't define fallback behavior when no pods exist or the most recent pod has been garbage-collected.
issues.suggestion: Specify fallback: 'If no agent pod with the /cache mount is currently running or recently terminated, omit the disk-usage section and display "Disk usage: unavailable (no recent pod)" instead.' Also clarify whether a new ephemeral pod should be spawned to inspect the PVC or if only existing/recent pods are queried.

issues.description: FR-6b references parsing 'SCCACHE_STATS:' lines from the log_events table, but no such parsing exists today and the spec does not define the expected line format, how to extract hit-rate numbers, or how to handle the case where sccache stats lines are absent (e.g., sccache not used or stats not emitted by the agent). The acceptance criteria (AC-6) requires 'hit-rate stats from recent loops' but the mechanism to capture those stats into log_events is not specified anywhere.
issues.suggestion: Either (a) define the SCCACHE_STATS line format and parsing logic (e.g., 'sccache emits JSON stats via --show-stats --stats-format json; the agent's log capture pipeline stores these in log_events with stage=implement'), or (b) descope hit-rate stats from FR-6 and make it a follow-up, since the log capture pipeline changes needed are non-trivial and not covered by this spec.

issues.description: Config merge strategy across layers is unspecified. The codebase uses a three-layer merge (cluster > repo > engineer) for all config. The spec only describes '[cache]' in nemo.toml (repo-level) but doesn't state whether cluster-level defaults or engineer-level overrides for cache config are supported, or how env maps merge (union? last-wins? repo-only?).
issues.suggestion: Add a sentence in FR-3: 'Cache config is repo-level only. The [cache] section is read from the repo's nemo.toml; cluster and engineer configs do not contribute cache settings. If future layers are needed, env maps merge with higher-priority layers winning per-key.' Or if multi-layer merge is desired, specify it.

issues.description: FR-1b says 'PVC is renamed in-place' and NFR-1 says 'terraform plans a rename,' but Terraform cannot rename a PVC in-place. Changing the PVC name in the Terraform manifest will produce a destroy-then-create plan, not a rename. This contradicts the acceptance criterion AC-5: 'Apply produces no destructive plan for the PVC (in-place rename, not replacement).' A PVC name change is inherently destructive in Terraform.
issues.suggestion: Acknowledge that the PVC name change IS destructive in Terraform and requires manual migration. Options: (a) use 'terraform state mv' to rename the resource in state, (b) import the existing PVC under the new resource name, or (c) accept data loss on cache (one cold refill). Update AC-5 to match the chosen strategy.

issues.description: The overview states the default size is 'bumped from #130's 20 GiB' to 50 GiB. However, the current Terraform variable 'cargo_cache_volume_size' already defaults to 50. Only the dev YAML (01-storage.yaml) has 20Gi. The narrative is slightly misleading about what's actually changing.
issues.suggestion: Clarify: 'Dev manifest bumped from 20 GiB to 50 GiB to match the existing Terraform default.'

issues.description: FR-6 (CLI command + API endpoint + log parsing + pod exec for disk usage) is a substantial feature that is architecturally distinct from the core cache volume/config work (FR-1 through FR-5). Bundling it in the same spec increases scope and implementation risk.
issues.suggestion: Consider noting FR-6 as a separable milestone that can be implemented and shipped independently from the core PVC/config changes. This doesn't require spec changes but would help implementation planning.
